decisions.reasoning: Added 1 share (5 total) ahead of WWDC 2026 on June 8. Wedbush set Street-high $400 PT citing AI monetization worth $75-100/share; BofA raised to $380 on agentic AI. WWDC expected to reveal Siri 2.0 with Gemini integration and on-device agent capabilities. Stock hit all-time closing high May 28 but today flat (+0.17%), so not a gap-up buy. Purchase takes gross long exposure to ~93%, within 95% cap. New avg entry $312.60.

sources.headline: WWDC26 keynote confirmed June 8, 10am PT — Siri 2.0 and Apple Intelligence AI platform
Analysts split on Apple stock ahead of June 8 AI reveal; Wedbush $400, BofA $380
Apple shares hit new all-time closing high

decisions.invalidation: WWDC AI announcements disappoint (Siri 2.0 underwhelms or gets delayed); stock breaks below $295 support

decisions.reasoning: Q1 FY2027 reported May 20: $81.6B revenue (+85% YoY), beat $78.8B consensus. Q2 guide $91B (+95% YoY), above $87.2B consensus. 62 analysts Strong Buy. Holding at 34.5% of equity — already trimmed 5 shares on May 28 to manage concentration. Bear case: hyperscaler custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Meta MTIA on Broadcom 2nm) eroding TAM, and Rubin delay creates timing uncertainty. Not adding given concentration already high.

sources.headline: Nvidia Q1 FY2027: Record $81.6B revenue, $1.87 EPS adj., Q2 guide $91B
Nvidia $20T thesis intact but hyperscaler custom silicon headwinds growing

decisions.invalidation: Any Mag-7 company walks back AI capex; AMD MI400 wins major hyperscaler contract away from NVDA; NVDA closes below $200 (slow SMA ~$212 now)

decisions.reasoning: Pentagon awarded $9.69B 5-year software deal to MSFT (May 28, Motley Fool/GuruFocus), stock up 3.4% today. Microsoft Build 2026 June 2-3 in San Francisco — new AI models, GitHub Copilot evolution, Azure AI Foundry updates. Strong upcoming catalyst. Cannot add yet: sold 2 shares on May 27 (2 days ago), 3-day churn rule prevents reversing. Will reassess post-Build.

sources.headline: Why Microsoft Stock Is Surging Today — Pentagon $9.69B deal
MSFT set to launch new AI models at Build conference next week (June 2-3)

decisions.invalidation: Build conference AI announcements disappoint; Pentagon deal gets legally challenged; Azure growth decelerates in next quarter

decisions.reasoning: S&P 500 at all-time highs; blended Q1 earnings growth 15.1% YoY, 84% beat rate. Forward P/E 20.9x elevated vs 19.9x 5-yr avg but RBC raised 12-mo target to 7,900 (vs ~7,570 current). Good core broad-market anchor.

sources.headline: May 2026 Economic Update: S&P 500 closed April at ATH 7,209, YTD +8.1%
RBC revamps S&P 500 target to 7,900 for rest of 2026

decisions.invalidation: Earnings growth decelerates sharply; Fed surprises hawkish; recession signals emerge

decisions.reasoning: Broad Nasdaq-100 exposure, bullish SMA signal (fast $719.67 > slow $691.43). Complements individual tech positions. Initiated May 28.
decisions.invalidation: Tech sector multiple compression; broad risk-off rotation

summary: First session: added 1 AAPL ahead of WWDC June 8 AI catalyst; held NVDA, MSFT, SPY, QQQ; portfolio +$86.76 (+0.87%) on the day
open_theses: AAPL: WWDC June 8 — Siri 2.0 + agentic AI reveal expected; Wedbush $400 PT on AI monetization thesis
MSFT: Build June 2-3 next catalyst; Pentagon $9.69B deal anchors enterprise AI narrative; reassess adding after 3-day rule clears May 30
NVDA: Post-earnings consolidation; strong fundamental case but monitoring hyperscaler custom silicon risk; may trim if concentration grows above 35%
SPY: Core broad-market hold through strong earnings cycle; watching for valuation compression signals
